name: arb-policies-skillset
description: Pull-mode pipeline for arb-policies: Content Understanding cracks the blob into markdown, SplitSkill chunks the markdown into pages, an AOAI chat skill categorizes each chunk, an AOAI embedding skill vectorizes each chunk, and index projections emit one chunk per index document.

skills.name: crack-to-markdown
skills.description: Content Understanding: extract the blob as a single markdown string. Chunking is delegated to SplitSkill below — CU's chunkingProperties output schema varies by API version, so we keep CU's job narrow (text extraction) and use the well-documented SplitSkill for chunking.

skills.name: split-into-pages
skills.description: Chunk the extracted markdown into ~3500-char pages with 200-char overlap. Output `pages` is an array of strings — downstream skills iterate `/document/pages/*`.

skills.name: categorize-chunk
skills.description: Assign each chunk a canonical PolicyCategory. Prompt is rendered from agents.categories.CATEGORIZE_SYSTEM_PROMPT; build_indexer.py substitutes the prompt at deploy time so the on-the-wire skillset stays byte-stable.

skills.name: embed-chunk
skills.description: Vectorize each chunk via the Foundry embeddings deployment. text-embedding-3-large is native 3072 dims — the index schema's contentVector field is 3072 to match. We deliberately do NOT pass `dimensions` here: the skill's `dimensions` field is a projection hint that the underlying deployment may silently ignore, leaving 3072-dim vectors flowing into a 1536 field (silent index-projection failure, empty index). Letting the model emit its native 3072 keeps the contract honest end-to-end.

cognitiveServices.description: Billing target for Content Understanding (CU) and chat-completion skills. Defaults to FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT (foundry-cc-canada). If CU is not supported in your Foundry region, set FOUNDRY_CU_ENDPOINT to an AI Services account in a CU-supported region (Sweden Central, East US 2, West US 3).
